LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LAMONS GASKET COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY WHICH FAILS TO SET FORTH A BASIS ON WHICH PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO A PRODUCT MANUFACTURED, SOLD, DISTRIBUTED OR PRODUCED BY POWER ENGINEERING OR LAMMONS. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. ALL PLAINTIFF IS ABLE TO DO IS TO ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF WORKED WITH FLEXITALLIC GASKETS BUT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO OFFER ANY EVIDENCE THAT POWER ENGINEERING SOLD THE GASKETS THAT PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO. THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF WAS ON A SHIP FOR THREE MONTHS IN CALIFORNIA AT A NAVAL BASE DOES NOT SHOW THAT HE WAS THERE DURING THE PERIOD THAT POWER ENGINEERING WAS THE EXCLUSIVE DISTRIBUTOR OR THAT PLAINTIFF DID ANY WORK ON SUCH GASKETS AT MARE ISLAND OR THAT ANY FLEXITALLIC GASKETS ON BOARD THE USS TULARE WERE SUPPLIED BY POWER ENGINEERING. GIVEN THIS RULING, THE COURT NEED NOT ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF SUCCESSOR LIABILITY. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO POWER ENGINEERING'S GO 129 INTERROGATORIES IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OR ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LAMONS GASKET COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: LAUREL HALBANY, ESQUIRE (BRAYTON PURCELL, 415.898.1555) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JANE MITCHELL WESTON, ESQUIRE (VASQUEZ & ESTRADA, 415.453.0555) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on plaintiff's factually devoid discovery which fails to set forth a basis on which plaintiff was exposed to a product manufactured, sold, distributed or produced by Power Engineering or Lammons. Plaintiff's evidence does not create a triable issue of fact. All plaintiff is able to do is to establish that plaintiff worked with Flexitallic gaskets but plaintiff is unable to offer any evidence that Power Engineering sold the gaskets that plaintiff was exposed to. The fact that plaintiff was on a ship for three months in California at a naval base does not show that he was there during the period that Power Engineering was the exclusive distributor or that plaintiff did any work on such gaskets at Mare Island or that any Flexitallic gaskets on board the USS Tulare were supplied by Power Engineering. Given this ruling, the Court need not address the issue of successor liability. Defendant's objection to Power Engineering's GO 129 interrogatories is overruled. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OR ORDER. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2008 Off Calendar
